title: Constitutional AI: Harmlessness from AI Feedback
summary: Introduced Constitutional AI (CAI), a method for training AI systems to be helpful, harmless, and honest using a set of explicit principles (a 'constitution') rather than relying solely on human feedback. The model critiques and revises its own responses based on these principles, then is trained via RLHF on AI-generated feedback. This reduces the need for human labelers to evaluate harmful content and provides a more transparent, auditable alignment methodology.
key_contributions: Introduced principle-based AI alignment — a 'constitution' guides model behavior
Reduced reliance on human labelers for safety-related feedback
Made AI alignment more transparent and auditable
Demonstrated that AI-generated feedback can effectively train harmlessness
impact: Established an alternative to pure RLHF for AI alignment. The constitutional approach — explicit principles guiding model behavior — influenced how the industry thinks about AI safety and governance. It represents Anthropic's core philosophical contribution to the field.
query_hooks: How do you make AI safe?
